final_answer:
Only one reason to keep them: test-only mocks let us force rare `GiltConsensus` failure paths deterministically without running a full live consensus stack for every test.

Why that matters for Gold Chain: if you delete mocks without replacement, coverage on edge/failure behavior drops or tests become very slow/flaky, which raises regression risk in production chain logic.

What should be done next:
1. If your policy is zero mocks, delete them all and convert those tests to real `GiltConsensus` integration tests.
2. If your policy is speed + coverage, keep only minimal mocks in `_test.go`/test helpers and ban any mock code from runtime packages.
3. Tell me which policy you want, and I will apply it exactly.
